case.title: WYCOFF CO., v. PUBLIC SERVICE COMMISSION OF UTAH

opinion.h1: WYCOFF CO., v. PUBLIC SERVICE COMMISSION OF UTAH, 379 U.S. 7 (1964)
opinion.h4: WYCOFF CO., INC. v. PUBLIC SERVICE COMMISSION OF UTAH ET AL. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF UTAH. No. 238. Decided October 12, 1964.
opinion.p: Appeal dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
A. Pratt Kesler, Attorney General of Utah, H. Wright Volker, Assistant Attorney General, and Keith E. Sohm for appellees.
The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
opinion.h4: AGEE v. COLUMBUS BAR ASSOCIATION, 379 U.S. 7 (1964)
opinion.p: 379 U.S. 7 AGEE v. COLUMBUS BAR ASSOCIATION. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF OHIO. No. 259. Decided October 12, 1964.
George E. Tyack and Alexander H. Martin, Jr., for appellant.
The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed for want of jurisdiction. Treating the papers whereon the appeal was taken as a petition for writ of certiorari, certiorari is denied.
